title: malephora luteola
intro: Malephora luteola, also known as the yellow stonecrop, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malephora luteola include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of Malephora luteola can be made by infusing the leaves or stems in hot water to create a tea or by making a salve or ointment by extracting the plant's oils and resins.
